prevalence.source: WHO European Regional Obesity Report 2022; Eurostat Health Interview Survey

incidence.source: WHO Europe; European Association for the Study of Obesity (EASO) estimates

burden.dalyPerCapita: 150-200 DALYs per 1,000 population attributable to obesity
burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 (IHME); WHO Europe

riskFactors: Unhealthy diet high in sugars and fats
vulnerablePopulations: Ethnic minorities (e.g., higher rates in Southern and Eastern Europe)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: BMI ≥ 30 kg/m² for adults; WHO classification (Class I: 30-34.9, Class II: 35-39.9, Class III: ≥40)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Routine BMI screening in primary care; coverage 60-80% in Western EU, lower (40-60%) in Eastern EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 20-30% achieve 5-10% sustained weight loss with interventions

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Standard WHO BMI criteria applied uniformly across EU member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Multidisciplinary: lifestyle modifications (diet/exercise), pharmacotherapy (e.g., GLP-1 agonists), bariatric surgery for severe cases; follows EASO guidelines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Socioeconomic disparities (higher prevalence in Eastern/Southern EU, e.g., 25-30% vs. 15-20% in Northern); rising childhood obesity (WHO target: halt increase by 2025); food environment influences
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: WHO European Action Plan for the Prevention and Control of NCDs (2016-2025) includes obesity targets; EU Joint Action on Nutrition and Physical Activity; national policies in countries like Denmark and Finland with high screening/treatment access
